content.full_text:
FAIR p OLITICAL p RACTICES COMMISSION 
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329 
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886 
Nancy Teicheira 
Ed Fichtner 
Manteca Unified School District 
27607 South Airport 
Manteca, CA 95337 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. W-11-101 
Dear Ms. Teicheira and Mr. Fichtner: 
May 26, 2011 
This letter confirms the receipt of your May 23, 2011, request for advice regarding 
Ms. King and Mr. Messer of the Manteca Unified School District. While the Commission's 
Legal Division provides advice to public officials with respect to their duties under the Political 
Reform Act, under the provisions of California Code of Regulations 18329 we cannot advise 
third parties about a public official's duties, unless that third party is the authorized 
representative of the official whose duties are in question. (Section 83114(a); Regulation 
18329(b)(8)(B) and (c)(l), copy enclosed.) Therefore, we are withdrawing your request. 
Should you have any additional questions regarding this matter, please contact the 
Commission at (916) 322-5660. 
JWW:jgl 
Enclosures 
Sincerely, 
/ 
A sistant General Counsel

sections.conclusion: We are withdrawing your request. The Commission's Legal Division provides advice to public officials with respect to their duties under the Political Reform Act, but under California Code of Regulations 18329 cannot advise third parties about a public official's duties, unless that third party is the authorized representative of the official whose duties are in question.
sections.question_synthetic: Can the FPPC provide advice to third parties regarding a public official's duties under the Political Reform Act?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC is withdrawing the request because it cannot provide advice to third parties about a public official's duties unless the third party is an authorized representative of that official.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Withdrawal letter with clear explanation of jurisdictional limitation. No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION sections present, but the substance and reasoning are clearly stated in the body of the letter.

QUESTION: Can the FPPC provide advice to third parties regarding a public official's duties under the Political Reform Act?

CONCLUSION: We are withdrawing your request. The Commission's Legal Division provides advice to public officials with respect to their duties under the Political Reform Act, but under California Code of Regulations 18329 cannot advise third parties about a public official's duties, unless that third party is the authorized representative of the official whose duties are in question.

embedding.summary: The FPPC received a May 23, 2011 request for advice regarding Ms. King and Mr. Messer of the Manteca Unified School District, but is withdrawing the request because it cannot advise third parties about public officials' duties under the Political Reform Act unless they are authorized representatives of those officials.
